title: Returning Every Other Row
notes: SQL rows have no inherent position, so ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY ename) manufactures one deterministically. Keeping the odd row numbers (rn % 2 = 1) returns every other row: the 1st, 3rd, 5th and so on. The % modulo operator is portable across all five engines; MOD() is not (SQL Server lacks it).
